lede: A look at June 14 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Steffi Graf and Alan Carr, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Grenfell Tower fire
events.desc: The Grenfell Tower fire, a catastrophic fire in a high-rise apartment building in North Kensington, London, UK, leaves 72 people dead and another 74 injured.

events.title: Falklands War
events.desc: Falklands War: Argentine forces in the capital Stanley conditionally surrender to British forces.

events.title: Chile
events.desc: Chile becomes a signatory to the Buenos Aires copyright treaty.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The German occupation of Paris begins.

events.title: Soviet ultimatum to Lithuania
events.desc: The Soviet Union presents an ultimatum to Lithuania, resulting in Lithuanian loss of independence.

events.title: Australian Eastern Mission
events.desc: The landmark Australian Eastern Mission returns from its three-month tour of East and South-East Asia.

events.title: Brazil
events.desc: Brazil leaves the League of Nations.

events.title: German Naval Laws
events.desc: The second German Naval Law calls for the Imperial German Navy to be doubled in size, resulting in an Anglo-German naval arms race.

events.title: Second Continental Congress
events.desc: The Second Continental Congress passes the Flag Act of 1777 adopting the Stars and Stripes as the Flag of the United States.

events.title: Franco-Spanish War (1635–1659)
events.desc: Franco-Spanish War: Turenne and the French army win a decisive victory over the Spanish at the battle of the Dunes.

deaths.text: Kurt Waldheim, Secretary-General of the United Nations, Austrian politician, 9th President of Austria
Jorge Luis Borges, Argentine short-story writer, essayist, poet and translator

observances.text: Christian feast day: Elisha (Roman Catholic and Lutheran)
Christian feast day: Fortunatus of Naples (Roman Catholic)
Christian feast day: Blessed Francisca de Paula de Jesus (Nhá Chica)
Christian feast day: Methodios I of Constantinople
Christian feast day: Richard Baxter (Church of England)
